rss.title: Musk accuses OpenAI lawyer of trying to 'trick' him in combative testimony
rss.snippet: Elon Musk was cross-examined on the third day of the trial over his lawsuit against Sam Altman and OpenAI.

rss.title: Why friendly AI chatbots might be less trustworthy
rss.snippet: Researchers found adjusting AI systems to be more warm and friendly to users would result in an "accuracy trade-off".

rss.title: Musk accuses Altman of stealing a charity as courtroom battle begins
rss.snippet: The case over OpenAI's history and public commitments could have major implications for the future of AI.

arxiv_papers.title: HalluCiteChecker: A Lightweight Toolkit for Hallucinated Citation Detection and Verification in the Era of AI Scientists
arxiv_papers.snippet: We introduce HalluCiteChecker, a toolkit for detecting and verifying hallucinated citations in scientific papers. arxiv_papers.title: From Black-Box Confidence to Measurable Trust in Clinical AI: A Framework for Evidence, Supervision, and Staged Autonomy
arxiv_papers.snippet: Trust in clinical artificial intelligence (AI) cannot be reduced to model accuracy, fluency of generation, or overall positive user impression. In medicine, trust must be engineered as a measurable system property grounded in evidence, supervision, and operational boundaries of AI autonomy. This art
